skills.description: Dynamo / Python scripting, structured data, Excel
Blender, Unreal Engine, AI-assisted design tools

transferList.to: Designing experiences through space → designing experiences through interfaces
Precision and clarity → Building thoughtful design systems

name: Koh Mei Qi
aboutParagraphs: Over the past few years, I've worked in architecture, designing and coordinating complex projects where clarity, usability, and human experience matter just as much as aesthetics. My background has taught me to think in systems, navigate constraints, and translate complexity into experiences that people can intuitively understand and use.
I believe every designed experience — whether a building, an interface, or an object — reflects a way of thinking about people.

My work and interests are shaped by curiosity: how people make decisions, how environments influence behavior, and how complexity can be transformed into something intuitive. I'm drawn to the tension between logic and emotion, structure and spontaneity, precision and ambiguity.
This space is less a portfolio and more an ongoing record of exploration — a collection of questions, experiments, and ideas that continue to shape how I think about design.
heroLede: I'm Koh Mei Qi — a former architectural designer moving into UX/UI design. Years of designing spaces, systems, and experiences for people have shaped how I think about usability, clarity, and human-centered design. Today, I'm applying that same mindset to digital products — creating experiences that are intuitive, meaningful, and thoughtfully crafted.

webShots.caption: Camera scan with real-time leaf analysis and condition scoring
Plant profile — habitat preferences and care conditions at a glance
Time-lapse slider: see how your plant fills out with good care

caseStudies.concept:
Most plant care apps are reference tools — you search, you scroll, you guess. Flora starts from a different premise: what if your plant could just tell you what's wrong?

The idea was to design a conversational AI companion that greets you by name, asks to see your plant through the camera, and reads it the way a knowledgeable friend might — pointing out what looks good, flagging what doesn't, and explaining what to do about it in plain language. No menus to navigate, no Latin names to look up. Just a conversation.

The visual language draws from scientific field guides and botanical illustration — precise, considered, and calm — but the interaction itself is warm and alive, built around motion, typing animations, and a camera scan that feels like something is genuinely paying attention. The AR growth timeline at the end lets you fast-forward your plant months or years into the future, so care stops feeling abstract and starts feeling worth it.
caseStudies.reflection: Note: This project is a high-fidelity interactive prototype demonstrating the app's design, user flow, and visual language. The camera scan, AI diagnosis, and AR growth preview are simulated interactions — the live plant analysis and augmented reality features are not yet technically implemented. The prototype is intended to communicate the intended experience, not to function as a finished product.
caseStudies.process: Conversational onboarding → camera scan → part-by-part analysis → care plan → growth preview.
caseStudies.outcome: A high-fidelity, clickable prototype spanning mobile app and responsive web.

mobileShots.caption: Camera scan with real-time leaf analysis and condition scoring
Plant profile — habitat preferences and care conditions at a glance
Time-lapse slider: see how your plant fills out with good care

caseStudies.title: Project 01 — Flora
caseStudies.description: A conversational app and responsive website that helps people diagnose issues with their houseplants. Scan a plant, get a living diagnosis with care advice, and fast-forward its growth in an AR-style timeline. Built as a fully interactive hi-fi prototype.

features.title: Conversational greeting
features.detail: Flora opens with a typed greeting that adapts to the time of day — good morning, afternoon, or evening — addressing you as "leaf whisperer." The interface feels like starting a conversation, not opening an app.

features.title: Camera scan with live analysis
features.detail: Point your camera at any houseplant and Flora scans it in real time. Annotated bubbles appear directly on the plant — flagging leaf condition, stem health, and flower state — so you can see exactly what Flora is reading and why.

features.title: Species identification
features.detail: Flora identifies your plant's species and growth stage from the scan, displaying a confidence match percentage. Works even if you're not sure what plant you have — just point and let it figure out.

features.title: Plain-language diagnosis
features.detail: Based on what it sees, Flora delivers a verdict in plain language — not a list of symptoms but a clear explanation of what's wrong and why, written the way a knowledgeable friend would tell you.

features.title: Personalised care plan
features.detail: Every diagnosis comes with a specific, actionable care plan — what to do, in what order, and how soon. No generic advice; the steps are tied directly to what Flora observed in your plant.

features.title: AR growth timeline
features.detail: Scrub through a time slider to preview how your plant will grow — days, weeks, months, or years ahead — with the plant's shape and size changing in real time. A signature feature that makes long-term care feel tangible and worth committing to.

features.title: Save to my plants
features.detail: After a diagnosis, save your plant to a personal collection. Flora tracks each plant's history and care schedule, and reminds you when it's time to check in again.

caseStudies.problem: Plant owners can tell something's wrong but rarely know what — or how to fix it before it's too late.

caseStudies.concept:
Most finance apps are designed around accounting principles. How Much To Live was designed around human behavior.

The project began with my own frustration: I wanted a system that could adapt to the way I actually spend, share, and think about money. By combining customizable categories, cash flow tracking, shared expenses, and multi-currency support, the goal was to create a financial tracker that reflects real-life complexity rather than forcing users into predefined structures.
caseStudies.process: Mapping personal finance behaviors → designing flexible categories, cash flow tracking, shared expenses, and multi-currency support.
caseStudies.outcome: A customisable finance tracker that transforms complex spending data into clear, actionable insights.
caseStudies.title: Project 02 — How Much To Live
caseStudies.description: A personal finance tracker inspired by my black cat, exploring the question: "How much does it actually cost to live?"

features.title: Flexible Category System
features.detail: Create personalised categories and subcategories that adapt to individual spending habits.

features.title: Monthly Cash Flow Tracking
features.detail: Track actual monthly cash flow by combining income, credit card payments, and daily expenses.

features.title: Shared Expense Management
features.detail: Record and organise shared spending with partners while maintaining individual financial visibility.

features.title: Multi-Currency Support
features.detail: Log expenses in different currencies and view them within a unified financial overview.

features.title: Payment Method Tracking
features.detail: Analyse spending patterns across cash, cards, bank transfers, and digital payments.

features.title: Automatic Spending Insights
features.detail: Automatically rank spending categories to reveal where money goes each month.

caseStudies.problem: Existing budgeting apps are often too rigid for real-life financial habits.
